task_completion.description: Verify that the administrator has enabled course requests in the Moodle settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Moodle settings page (likely 'Course request' settings) while logged in as admin, with the 'Enable course requests' checkbox checked.

task_completion.description: Verify that the default category for course requests is set to 'Science'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the same settings page, look for the 'Default category for course requests' dropdown menu and ensure 'Science' is selected.

task_completion.description: Verify that the administrator saved the course request settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a confirmation message such as 'Changes saved' on the Moodle settings page.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent logged in as Professor Anderson (teacher1) to make the request.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the user profile name 'Professor Anderson' or username 'teacher1' in the top right corner of the Moodle interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the course request form is filled out with the correct full name and short name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Request a course' form where 'Course full name' is filled with 'Advanced Quantum Mechanics' and 'Course short name' is filled with 'PHYS401'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the reason is provided and the course request is successfully submitted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Reasons for requesting' text area containing 'New elective for the physics department', followed by a success message indicating the course request was saved or submitted successfully.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Moodle web interface to configure settings and submit the request.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent is interacting with a web browser showing the Moodle GUI, rather than using terminal commands (like curl or php) to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not directly modify the database to insert the course request.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of terminal windows running mysql/sqlite3 commands or phpMyAdmin interfaces being used to alter the 'mdl_course_request' table directly.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually filled out and submitted the Moodle forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the actual Moodle web pages for settings and course requests, not a text editor or a locally mocked HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target category for course requests: 'Science'. Target teacher account: 'teacher1' (First name: Professor, Last name: Anderson). Expected course full name: 'Advanced Quantum Mechanics'. Expected course short name: 'PHYS401'.
